sources: AIGA (American Institute of Graphic Arts) Design Careers
Creative Mornings and Design Industry Surveys
NASAD (National Association of Schools of Art and Design) Standards
Art Directors Club and One Club Career Resources
LinkedIn Creative and Design Hiring Trends

smart.label: Academic Rigor and Craft
what_counts: Art, Design, Film, Music, Theatre, or related major
Relevant coursework: design theory, art history, production, technique
Honors, awards, or scholarships in the discipline
Academic distinction (dean's list, portfolio review recognition)

grit.label: Portfolio and Productions
what_counts: Exhibitions, performances, screenings, or shows with dates and venue
Production roles with clear ownership (director, designer, lead)
Quantified impact: audience size, reach, collaborations
Freelance or client work with deliverables
Sustained commitment to a body of work

build.label: Concrete Work Evidence
what_counts: Portfolio link with specific projects named
Projects with client, venue, or context
Roles in productions: design, direction, performance, technical
Collaborations with named artists, orgs, or brands
Tools and mediums: Adobe Suite, Figma, Pro Tools, etc.
Outcomes: awards, audience reach, publication, or distribution

common_gaps.gap: No portfolio link or work samples
common_gaps.fix: Portfolio is non-negotiable. Create a simple site (Behance, Cargo, Squarespace). Include 5-8 strongest projects with context, role, and outcome.

common_gaps.gap: Projects lack context, role, or outcome
common_gaps.fix: Every project needs: what it was, your role, who it was for (or 'personal'), and the outcome (audience, award, client feedback). Name the medium and tools.
Seek opportunities: student shows, local galleries, theatre productions, film festivals, design competitions. Even one public presentation is a signal.

common_gaps.gap: Resume reads like a job seeker, not a creative
common_gaps.fix: Use discipline-specific language. Name your medium, style, or focus. List relevant coursework and awards. Show you think like a practitioner.
Add numbers where possible: audience size, followers, collaborators, pieces in show, run length. 'Exhibited in group show of 8 artists' beats 'exhibited work.'
